# Questions in Livestream 15

Live Stream #15: And Other Taboo Jewish Numbers

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/s3grFd_g6yk?t=339" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">5:39</a> | How does the *Instruction of Merikare* saying that humans are "the likeness of god" relate to Genesis 1:26? | He said the Egyptian text is not the same doctrine as the image of God, though both can speak about humans becoming god-like or wielding god-like power. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/s3grFd_g6yk?t=632" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">10:32</a> | Would Egyptian contextual understanding still have shaped Jews in Jesus's day? | Only faintly. He said the original Egyptian background had largely dropped out by the first century, even though core traditions like Passover remained. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/s3grFd_g6yk?t=850" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">14:10</a> | Which active archaeological site looks most promising, and which site is being overworked? | He highlighted places like Tell el-Retaba and Carchemish as promising, and criticized the continued excavation of Shiloh as beating a dead horse. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/s3grFd_g6yk?t=1225" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">20:25</a> | How much Egyptian culture remained after Hellenization? | He said Hellenization was strong, especially in the north, but Egyptian culture also resisted it and continued alongside Greek culture. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/s3grFd_g6yk?t=1451" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">24:11</a> | Is there a published original-language edition of the Dead Sea Scrolls? | Yes. He recommended the *Discoveries in the Judaean Desert* series, while noting that it is excellent but extremely expensive. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/s3grFd_g6yk?t=1668" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">27:48</a> | Were there human sacrifices in ancient Egypt and Mesopotamia, and when did they end? | Yes, in both. He cited royal retainers, prisoners of war, and possible foundation deposits, but said the evidence is limited and did not give a firm end date. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/s3grFd_g6yk?t=1804" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">30:04</a> | Are the Dead Sea Scrolls basically the same as the Bible we have today? | Broadly yes at the doctrinal level, but they preserve meaningful textual variants and show multiple textual traditions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/s3grFd_g6yk?t=1984" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">33:04</a> | What books does he recommend on Egyptian or ancient Near Eastern chronology? | He chiefly recommended *Ancient Egyptian Chronology* edited by Erik Hornung, Rolf Krauss, and David Warburton. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/s3grFd_g6yk?t=2190" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">36:30</a> | What path does he recommend for advanced study in Old Testament, New Testament, or patristics, and should it be online? | He strongly advised in-person study, warned against trying to master all three tracks at once, and said graduate training depends heavily on relationships and departmental fit. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/s3grFd_g6yk?t=2540" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">42:20</a> | Is Redford recent enough to count as current scholarship, and should pessimistic induction be applied to the current field? | He said speculative theories can become dated quickly, while primary data and transcriptions age better; scholars need to keep reading and test claims against current evidence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/s3grFd_g6yk?t=2940" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">49:00</a> | For Old Testament doctoral work, should the focus be the university or the supervisor? | The supervisor. He said students should identify the scholar they want to study under first, then evaluate the department and institution. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/s3grFd_g6yk?t=3337" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">55:37</a> | Why does he call Ron Wyatt a charlatan? | Because Wyatt made sensational claims without real evidence, and Falk argued that such stories can badly damage people's faith when they collapse. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/s3grFd_g6yk?t=3690" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:01:30</a> | What does he think of the Hobby Lobby antiquities debacle? | He said the Museum of the Bible was a worthwhile goal pursued badly; the acquisitions should have been handled lawfully and more carefully. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/s3grFd_g6yk?t=3958" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:05:58</a> | Why does modern biblical studies often seem more speculative than Egyptology or Assyriology? | He said biblical studies leans more heavily on secondary literature and inherited academic tradition, while the other fields are often more tightly tied to primary data. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/s3grFd_g6yk?t=4359" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:12:39</a> | Is online undergraduate work acceptable if someone hopes to do in-person postgraduate study later? | He said it can be done, but it still weakens later preparation and admissions compared with doing as much as possible in person. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/s3grFd_g6yk?t=4750" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:19:10</a> | Which ancient Egyptian temple is his favorite? | He chose the Temple of Seti I at Abydos, with Luxor and Dendera also receiving praise. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/s3grFd_g6yk?t=5052" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:24:12</a> | Can he break down the Egyptian word *heka* and its usage, and clarify the seated-god determinative? | He said *heka* can refer to magic in several forms, while determinatives are unpronounced signs; the seated-god determinative is not *heka* and is mostly used with male deities or divinized dead kings. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/s3grFd_g6yk?t=5487" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:31:27</a> | Can we know whether the Jerubbaal inscription refers to Gideon? | No. He said the identification is possible and even plausible, but still remains speculative. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/s3grFd_g6yk?t=5680" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:34:40</a> | Does he choose non-super-chat questions from the top, the bottom, or by another method? | He said he does not have a fixed method; he jumps around and chooses non-super-chat questions that look interesting. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/s3grFd_g6yk?t=5762" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:36:02</a> | Can Joseph's time as vizier be inferred from the transfer of land to the king in Egypt, and did that become permanent? | He said that pattern fits the Hyksos period well and points to Joseph in the Second Intermediate Period, though later landholding became more mixed. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/s3grFd_g6yk?t=6049" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:40:49</a> | Did ordinary Egyptians move around a lot, or stay in the same house for life? | He said poorer farming families often shifted between high ground and floodplain seasonally, while elites could remain more settled. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/s3grFd_g6yk?t=6208" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:43:28</a> | What are his thoughts on *Neon Genesis Evangelion* and favorite anime more broadly? | He said Gundam is his clear favorite, while *Evangelion* never connected with him in the same way. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/s3grFd_g6yk?t=6446" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:47:26</a> | How did kingship develop as an institution? | He linked it to settled agrarian society, irrigation management, storage, and the need to protect the food supply. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/s3grFd_g6yk?t=6727" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:52:07</a> | What are the earliest Egyptian writings that discuss Greece? | He said Egypt recognized Greece by the New Kingdom, but Egyptian literature was generally not very interested in detailed foreign ethnography. |
